channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Move over, Harness Engineering, it is time for the harness of harnesses!

item.title: Volcano Engine Is Mass-Producing Opus 4.6-Level Models as ByteDance's AI Factory Goes Into Overdrive
item.description: ByteDance's Volcano Engine AI platform has achieved the capability to mass-produce models competitive with Anthropic's Opus 4.6 at a fraction of the cost, marki...

item.title: ByteDance Doubao Paid Version Pushes WPS Into a Corner With Built-in AI-Powered Office Suite
item.description: Doubao's Pro tier includes native document, spreadsheet and presentation tools, threatening Kingsoft's WPS dominance in the AI era

item.title: ByteDance Doubao Launches Paid Subscription Up to 500 Yuan Per Month as AI Compute Costs Bite
item.description: Doubao's new Pro tier offers three pricing levels — 68, 200 and 500 yuan monthly — as daily AI compute costs exceed 132 million yuan

item.title: China's LineShine Supercomputer Surpasses the US, Tops Global TOP500 Rankings With Pure CPU Design
item.description: China's domestically-developed LineShine supercomputer reclaims the world's top spot using an all-CPU architecture without any GPU accelerators

item.title: Tungsten Hexafluoride: The Invisible King Behind Chip Tungsten Plugs and China's Domestic Substitution Drive
item.description: WF6 prices surge 232% YoY as Japanese producers exit, creating a 1000-ton supply gap that Chinese manufacturers race to fill

item.title: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
item.description: In a rare double-interview, the Databricks technical leaders riff on what it will take for every company to build Agent Clouds

item.title: Elevated errors on Opus 4.8 Fast
item.description: Jun 24, 18:33 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 24, 18:22 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps
item.description: From hidden content injections to cognitive state poisoning, attackers are turning trusted data sources into traps for autonomous AI. The post When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Issues with granular roles for Cloudflare Tunnel and Mesh
item.description: Jun 24, 17:30 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 24, 15:16 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating permission issues with resource-based granular roles where a subset of users are not being granted access to their scoped Cloudflare Tunnel and Cloudflare Mesh resources.This...

item.title: INC20000052
item.description: Jun 24, 17:22 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've implemented the fix for this issue and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 26, 2026. The vulnerability in...

item.title: Introducing computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash
Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel
Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
item.description: A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC. "The main common goal was to disrupt the 'assembly lines' cybercriminals...

item.title: Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants
item.description: Europe is in the middle of a record-breaking heat wave, and the grid is being pushed to its limits as people turn to fans and air-conditioning to try to stay cool. Some power plants won’t be online to help handle the load. On June 23, France saw its hottest day since record-keeping began in...

item.title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
item.description: Hundreds of C&C servers were disrupted in an operation involving law enforcement and several cybersecurity companies. The post Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
item.description: The new framework seeks to help security teams identify which software supply chain vulnerabilities pose the greatest operational, safety, and business risks in AI-driven environments. The post Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk appeared first on...

item.title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
item.description: A standard non-admin account is sufficient to conduct an attack that exploits legitimate OS behavior rather than software vulnerabilities. The post macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated error rate on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 24, 13:16 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison
item.description: Nathan Austad has been ordered to pay roughly $1.8 million in forfeiture and restitution, and the sentence also includes 3 years of supervised release. The post Third DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to 18 Months in Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains. The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of...

item.title: The Download: introducing the Engineering issue
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: the Engineering issue We can’t fix everything, but we can be ambitious. We can take on the challenge of making the world better through...

item.title: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
item.description: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new…

item.title: The emergence of the web data infrastructure layer for AI
item.description: AI is booming. New use cases are emerging each day. To capitalize on the technology’s potential, enterprises require data at scale. In many cases, though, the relevant information is blocked or unstructured, which limits its use by AI models.  To understand this challenge, consider the...

item.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
item.description: We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats. For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE was cataloged, a vendor navigated a patch cycle, and weeks or even months later, a fix was deployed....

item.title: This flying solar-powered platform could deliver better internet from the air
item.description: As soon as August, a giant silver bullet will cut its way through the dry air of the southwestern US and cross the Pacific to reach the coast of Japan.  Once there, the roughly 200-foot-long craft, built by the New Mexico–based company Sceye, will park some 18 kilometers above the ocean’s...

item.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Tuesday announced the seizure of a cloud computing account put to use by subsidiaries of Cambodia-based corporate conglomerate HuiOne Group, as the Treasury unveiled fresh sanctions against nine individuals and 26 entities linked to Prince Group. "These...

item.title: [AINews] Claude Tag: Multiplayer, Proactive, Persistent Agents in Slack
item.description: Claude finally gets a Slackbot upgrade

item.title: gpt-4o-mini high error rate
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Images (Operational) Chat Completions (Operational) Realtime (Operational) Embeddings (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Responses (Operational) Login (Operational) Batch (Operational) Audio (Operational)...

item.title: RIFT-Bench: Dynamic Red-teaming For Agentic AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.23927v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are rapidly evolving into autonomous decision-making systems, exposing attack vectors beyond those of traditional LLM vulnerabilities. Existing security evaluations are often tied to specific...

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic Drive: Rule-Grounded Faithful Reasoning for Driving VLAs
item.description: arXiv:2606.23938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Driving VLA models incorporating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning are attractive because they leverage pretrained VLM representations and expose intermediate decisions in natural language, yet current rationales often lack the step-by-step decision...

item.title: Critique of Agent Model
item.description: arXiv:2606.23991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What is an agent? What constitutes agency? With the rise of Large Language Model (LLM) systems marketed as ``coding agents'', ``AI co-scientists'', and other ``agentic" tools that promise to drive up productivity, and at the same time, ``existential"...

item.title: Safe and Generalizable Hierarchical Multi-Agent RL via Constraint Manifold Control
item.description: arXiv:2606.24010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems are widely used in safety-critical applications that require coordinated behavior under strict safety constraints. Existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off: learning-based methods achieve strong empirical performance but...

item.title: Reinforcement Learning Towards Broadly and Persistently Beneficial Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.24014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As AI systems are deployed across increasingly diverse and high-stakes settings, model alignment must generalize beyond the tasks and domains seen during training. This is especially important for reinforcement learning (RL), which can introduce...

item.title: Systematic Exploration of 4-Expert Heterogeneous Mixture-of-Experts via Automated Pipeline Search
item.description: arXiv:2606.23739v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present an automated large-scale search pipeline for heterogeneous 4-Expert Mixture-of-Experts (MoE4) architectures within the LEMUR neural network dataset ecosystem. Building on a hand-crafted heterogeneous MoE reference model, we replace manual...

item.title: Weight-Space Geometry of Offline Reasoning Training
item.description: arXiv:2606.23740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline reinforcement-learning losses (RFT, RIFT, DFT, Offline GRPO, DPO) are widely used to distill reasoning from large teachers into smaller students, and are typically compared on downstream accuracy alone. We ask whether they are mechanistically...

item.title: A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
item.description: arXiv:2606.23741v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal reasoning, which encompasses the discovery of causal structures and the inference of causal effects, is fundamental to data-driven decision making. In practice, data for reliable causal analysis are often distributed across institutions and...

item.title: Low-power analogue neural networks with trainable nonlinear connections for continuous control
item.description: arXiv:2606.23742v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physical neural networks promise low-power machine learning by computing directly with analogue device physics, but most architectures force nonlinear device responses to act as scalar weights. Inspired by Kolmogorov-Arnold networks, we place...

item.title: Synergizing Physically Constrained MCMC and Chemical-Informed Gaussian Processes for Reaction Network Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2606.23757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Extracting interpretable governing equations from sparse, noisy chemical time-series data remains difficult because discrete reaction topology and continuous kinetic parameters are tightly coupled. We present PC-MCMC-CIGP, a reproducible gray-box...

item.title: EXPO-SQL: Execution-based Clause-level Policy Optimization for Text-to-SQL
item.description: arXiv:2606.23693v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL enables users to query databases using natural language by generating executable SQL queries. Recent methods have increasingly adopted Large Language Models based reinforcement learning (RL) to leverage execution feedback for training....

item.title: ModTGCN: Modularity-aware Graph Neural Networks for Text Classification
item.description: arXiv:2606.23694v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph-based text classification models typically rely on local neighborhood aggregation and overlook global community structure, despite semantic document graphs exhibiting strong class-consistent clustering. Ignoring this can blur class boundaries...

item.title: Quantifying Prior Dominance in RAG Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.23695v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds Large Language Models in external knowledge, yet current evaluations rely on discrete heuristics that suffer from ''epistemic blindness'' - failing to distinguish genuine contextual information extraction...

item.title: Self-Recognition Finetuning can Prevent and Reverse Emergent Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2606.23700v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emergent misalignment (EM) has been linked to the activation of misaligned persona vectors and evil character traits, suggesting that EM operates through disruption of the model's aligned character rather than direct learning of harmful content....

item.title: Evaluating LLM Usage for Efficient and Explainable Numerical and Classified Implicit Sentiment Analysis of Product Desirability
item.description: arXiv:2606.23701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Qualitative product feedback can reveal nuanced user experiences, but its implicit sentiment is difficult to measure. This paper presents a scalable and interpretable framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to quantify product desirability...

item.title: Introducing the FFASR Leaderboard: Benchmarking ASR in the Real World
We are seeing elevated errors with Next Edit Suggestions and Completions
item.description: Jun 23, 23:29 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 23, 23:27 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to...

item.title: Claude.ai is experiencing elevated error rates
item.description: Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 23, 18:32 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 23, 18:24 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: INC20000047
item.description: Jun 23, 17:50 UTC Update - Current status: We're continuing to investigate the issue affecting Snowpark Container Services to determine its source. We'll provide another update within 1 hour.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified region may be intermittently unable to access or...

item.title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet they remain prone to generating hallucinations. Detecting these hallucinations is critical for deploying LLMs reliably in high-stakes applications. We present Grad Detect, a gradient-based approach for...
